prerequisites: A MotherDuck account with access to the MotherDuck UI at https://app.motherduck.com.
A MotherDuck access token (API key), created from your organization Settings. Required for the motherduck_api_key field.
A target MotherDuck database name to write to, supplied via destination_path using the md: connection format (e.g. md:my_database).

authMethods.name: Access token (API key)
authMethods.summary: MotherDuck authenticates programmatic clients with an access token, which maps to this connector's required motherduck_api_key secret field. Create the token in the MotherDuck UI under your organization Settings (+ Create token). MotherDuck offers Read/Write (default) and Read Scaling token types; use a Read/Write token for a destination that writes data.

setupSteps.title: Create a MotherDuck access token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the MotherDuck UI at https://app.motherduck.com. Click your organization name in the top left, open Settings, then click + Create token. Give the token a name, choose the token type (use Read/Write so the destination can write data), set expiration, click Create token, and copy the value.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token as motherduck_api_key
setupSteps.body: Paste the copied access token into the connector's required motherduck_api_key field. MotherDuck treats this token as your authentication credential for programmatic access; keep it secret.

setupSteps.title: Set the destination database (destination_path)
setupSteps.body: Use destination_path to point at your MotherDuck database with the md: format, for example md:my_database. Connecting with duckdb uses the same md:database_name syntax.

setupSteps.title: Optionally set the schema
setupSteps.body: Use the optional schema field to target a specific schema within the database. In MotherDuck the schema is selected separately from the database (for example via fully qualified table names like my_db.main.table or a USE statement); leave it at the default if you do not need a specific schema.

sources.title: Authenticating to MotherDuck (create an access token)
Connecting to MotherDuck (md: database connection format)
